utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey philip good to see you today so take a look here at my notes i see you're coming in for some right knee pain and you have a past medical history of hypertension and we will take a look at that so can you tell me what happened to your knee

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i was you know i was just doing some work on my property and i i accidentally slipped and fell down and i just still having some knee issues

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay well that that's not good do you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: what part of your knee would you say hurts

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i would just say you know the it it you know it basically when i when i'm flexing my knee when i'm moving it up and down and i put pressure on it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright did you hear a pop or anything like that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did feel something pop yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and did it was it swollen afterwards or is it looks a little bit swollen right now

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah little bit swollen yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so so far have you taken anything for the pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: just taking some ibuprofen just for some swelling

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay that's it what would you say your pain score is a out of ten with ten being the worst pain you ever felt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i would say that when i'm stationary i do n't really feel a lot of pain but if i start doing some mobility i would say probably a four five

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: about a four okay and how long ago did you say this was is this happened this injury

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's been a week now

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: a week okay alright alright so we will take a look i'll do a physical exam of your knee in a second but i do want to check up you do have a past medical history of hypertension i'm seeing here you're on twenty milligrams of lisinopril when you came in today your blood pressure was a little bit high it was one fifty over seventy so have you been taking your medications regularly

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes i have

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so you might have a little white coat syndrome i know some of my patients definitely do have that so what about your diet i know we talked a little bit before about you reducing your sodium intake to about twenty three hundred milligrams per per day i know you were during the pandemic your diet got out of little bit out of control so how have you been doing how have you been doing with that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i definitely need some help there i have not have not made some some changes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay yeah we definitely need to get you to lower that salt intake get your diet a little bit better because the hope is to get you off that medication and get your blood pressure to a manageable level okay so we yeah we definitely can talk about that alright so lem me take a look at your knee i'll do a quick physical exam on you and before i do just want to make sure you're not having any chest pain today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: are you any belly pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no shortness of breath just wan na make sure

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so i'm just gon na listen to your lungs here your lungs are clear bilaterally i do n't hear any wheezes or crackles listen to your heart so on your heart exam i do still hear that grade two out of six systolic ejection murmur and you already had that and so we we knew about that already so lem me look at your knee here so when i press here on the inside of your knee does that hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a little bit

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: little bit how about when i press on the outs the outside gon na press on the outside is that painful

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no alright so i'm gon na have you flex your knee is that painful

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah that's uncomfortable

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's uncomfortable and extend it so that's painful

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so on your knee exam i i see that you do have pain to palpation of the medial aspect of your right knee you have some pain with flexion extension i also identify some edema around the knee and some effusion you have a little bit of fluid in there as well so prior to coming in we did do an x-ray of that right knee and luckily you did n't break anything so there is no fractures no bony abnormalities so let's talk a little bit about my assessment and plan for you so you have what we call a mcl strain so a medial collateral ligament strain so when you fell i think you twisted a little bit and so it irritated you strained that that ligament there so for that what we can do for you first i'm gon na prescribe you some ibuprofen eight hundred milligrams and you can take that twice a day and that's gon na help you with that swelling and that pain that you currently do have i'm also gon na put you in a a knee brace just to try and support those muscles to allow it to heal and then i want you to ice the knee you can do that for twenty minutes at a time for three to four times a day that should also help with the the swelling of your knee for your hypertension now i'm gon na keep you on that twenty of lisinopril okay because you are taking it and you you're doing pretty good with it i also want to get you a referral to nutrition just to try to help you with that diet you know because right now you are your diet is little bit out of control so we just need to rain you in a little bit and hopefully you know with their help we can eventually get you off that lisinopril alright so do you have any questions for me

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: do i need to elevate my leg or stay off my leg or

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah i would yeah you can elevate your leg stay off your stay off your leg you know if you have any kids have them work out in the yard instead of you just to to for a couple of weeks it's a good thing if you want to do that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: tell him this doctor's order

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: tell definitely tell him his doctor tell him i said it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: alright do you have any other questions no that's it i appreciate you seeing me

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so my nurse will be in with the those orders and we will see you next time

detail_list.summary: The patient presents for right knee pain and follow-up for hypertension.
The patient reports a fall while working on his property one week ago.
The doctor asks the patient to identify the location of the knee pain.
The patient reports pain with flexion and extension and when applying pressure.
The doctor asks if the patient heard a pop during the injury.
The patient reports feeling a pop during the injury.
The doctor asks if the knee was swollen.
The patient reports the knee is swollen.
The doctor asks if the patient has taken any medication for the pain.
The patient has been taking ibuprofen for swelling.
The doctor asks the patient to rate their pain on a scale of one to ten.
The patient reports a pain score of four to five during mobility.
The doctor asks about the duration of the injury.
The injury occurred one week ago.
Hypertension
The doctor asks if the patient is taking their lisinopril regularly.
The patient confirms regular medication adherence.
The doctor asks about the patient's dietary habits and sodium intake.
The patient acknowledges difficulty with dietary changes.
The doctor asks if the patient is experiencing chest pain, abdominal pain, or shortness of breath.
The patient denies chest pain.
The patient denies abdominal pain.
The patient denies shortness of breath.
Physical exam shows clear lungs and a grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.
The doctor asks if the medial aspect of the knee is painful to palpation.
The patient reports pain on the medial aspect of the knee.
The doctor asks if the lateral aspect of the knee is painful to palpation.
The patient denies pain on the lateral aspect of the knee.
The doctor asks if knee flexion is painful.
The patient reports discomfort with flexion.
The doctor asks if knee extension is painful.
The patient reports pain with extension.

Physical exam findings include medial knee pain, effusion, and edema; X-rays show no fractures. The diagnosis is an MCL strain. The plan includes ibuprofen 800 mg twice daily, a knee brace, icing 20 minutes 3-4 times daily, continuing lisinopril 20 mg daily, and a nutrition referral.
The patient asks if they should elevate the leg or avoid activity.
The doctor instructs the patient to elevate the leg and avoid strenuous activity for 2-3 weeks.
